item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a perfect example of what the Prometheus Hub is stopping, not that all aliens are evil or ass-farts, well latter can be anyway, but that this is a race that lived so peacefully in life that they weren't aware of the dangers of evil in the last stand. I wish the ME games had a more mystic/ higher-being feel to the Reapers besides being genocidal machines like the terminator's, maybe I might do a proper fanfix on that? But for that, fanfic or Ao3?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is the 'dying messenger' trope but in space with biomechanical bits. I'm ok with this +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn’t this be classified as neutralised?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reply if you've heard that the Opportunity rover's last words were "My battery is low and it's getting dark."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it just me, or do lines 9-12 of "Lifelight" come to your mind when reading the message?

"Silence grows, in the spaces between
Stretching out beyond time
Rising up, as a chorus of souls finds a voice
Flickering through the void"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ye gods. An extraterrestrial SCP second only to "To the Makers of Music" in feels. Absolutely heartbreaking, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The feels, Mann; the feels…
This is a beautiful article and the discussion is pure comedy gold.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I may have cried slightly.

It was a good message.

item.title: Re: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: For more on this concept, see David Brin's Existence: https://www.amazon.com/Existence-David-Brin/dp/0765342626/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1522089259&sr=8-1&keywords=existence+brin

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Those sound like really cool ideas. Approved.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Okay, so, the blackbox SCP that gets mentioned here is something in the Kuiper belt that the Foundation is keeping an eye on. That matches SCP-2362 pretty well, so it could be linked from there. Additionally, the mention of the O5 Council here can be turned into a link to the O5 Dossier. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1281 is a biomechanical entity found in the Kuiper belt during standard containment of SCP-████.

To this:

SCP-1281 is a biomechanical entity found in the Kuiper belt during standard containment of SCP-2362.

Changing this:

SCP-1281 shut down for approximately four hours. The temperature around SCP-1281's dorsal bulge heated up significantly, to about 60 Kelvin. The Foundation team went into standby, in the event that SCP-1281 became hostile, while a message was transmitted back to Earth asking for further instructions. The O5 Council advised caution, but instructed Dr. Bloom to continue attempts to communicate, with an eye to bringing SCP-1281 into a containment solution.

To this:

SCP-1281 shut down for approximately four hours. The temperature around SCP-1281's dorsal bulge heated up significantly, to about 60 Kelvin. The Foundation team went into standby, in the event that SCP-1281 became hostile, while a message was transmitted back to Earth asking for further instructions. The O5 Council advised caution, but instructed Dr. Bloom to continue attempts to communicate, with an eye to bringing SCP-1281 into a containment solution.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Re: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: I'm also necroposting here, but oh my god, that was one of the greatest comments I've even seen. A very well written SCP too. Way more emotional than I expected at first.

item.title: Re: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: Don't care that I'm necroposting, this comment was just too good

item.title: Re: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: Two and a half years late to the party, but this comment is 100% pure gold. #bless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*sniffles* It's a love note from aliens ;~;
A stark contrast from the usual 'aleenz keel us' trope, very nicely written

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once I get the syth down for Harbinger, you'd better believe I'm making audio versions of those interview logs.

If I can control my feels long enough to do it.

+1.

And curse you for sending me on this interstellar feels trip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One light fades, another kindles, and the cold blank universe knows itself again.

This is beautiful. *sniffles*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm Commander Shepard, and this is my favorite SCP on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't get that impression from it at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's what makes it good (as I said earlier).
It's a pointless "reblog for support" on an intergalactic scale. And the little drone feels it's accomplished something, while the something is actually trite and stupid.

Think of the original guy who ran from the battle at Marathon to Athens, if instead of a message of victory, he delivered something like "Shop at Theofanos Nikiforias today for 40% discount on goat cheese!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I'm just a cold, cynical ass, because I thought this was the sappiest thing since sap came to Sap Town. And it didn't even have anything important to say? Just "Keep on keepin' on"? Jee thanks Befuddled Alien Object, if it weren't for your message, we woulda just sat around and let bugs eat our eyes out rather than trying to survive and flourish

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Welp. Right in the feels. Take your +1, you magnificent bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my new favorite SCP. This is just… wonderful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like for Stars

Share for Planets

Comment for Asteroids

Ignore for the cold void of space

item.title: Re: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: Greatest thing yet written on the site? Emotional wreck that I became after reading the SCP, I barked in sudden laughter, drawing the attention of my colleagues to my slightly tear-stained face…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can I doublepluss-upvote x2?

Pleeeeaaase?

P.S. I wish this were found in the asteroid belt, or somewhere closer to Earth, seeing as getting people to the Kuiper Belt would be financially… is wack an acceptable sentiment? Communication could also be made via a probe like New Horizons or somesuch, but needless to say this nitpick is heavily outweighed by the rest of this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have much to say that hasn't already been said. A strongly upbeat existential skip is best skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I only just now saw this SCP. That was my exact reaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, this is only the second FOUNDATION IN THE FUTURE scp I've read (the other being the 682 eggs), but I like this much better. Heck, if it was in the asteroid belt and they were checking 1958 (the vw bus in space), I feel that this could be modern day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only signs that it was active were faint lights running along its surface.

Mechanical lights, or bioluminescent?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HNNNNNNNNG.
My feels have been set alight.
Well-written, +1.

item.title: Re: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: Somehow, I feel like this entire article exists simply so this single joke could be made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This post pretty much sums up my feelings. It was hard to get past the fact that we have an outpost beyond Neptune to start. During the interview logs, all I could think of was how long it would take to communicate with Earth at that distance (8 hours to send a message and receive a response if I'm not mistaken). However, if we're assuming this is an article from the future where we have FTL communication and very fast spacecrafts, then I guess I can look past that.

Even so, the rest doesn't do it for me. No vote, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for pointing that out. I fixed it for Mann.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Point of order: "degrees Kelvin" is inaccurate. "Kelvins" is the unit of measurement of the Kelvin temperature scale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What is this fluttering in my chest? I….I don't understand it….
Anyways, very well written, bravo. My upvote, this one has

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote for everything but the very cheesy last few lines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me very, very sad. +1 for making me sad for a robot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't really get anything out of it. The interview -4 reminded me of the talk from Top 10 ("Does that mean they're losing?" "No. First there was only darkness."), itself probably derived from something else. It struck me as trying to hard to elicit feels. It's well-written feel-bait, but feel-bait nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just feel that this is at its core a lesser retread of SCP-1342, except the probe itself is alive.

item.title: Re: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: this is glory

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The message is really the problem here. It's superbly written, but feels… derivative. Unfortunately I can't be specific as to what I feel it's derivative //of //

Vonnegut's Sirens of Titan?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(I absolutely love the spam/reblog stuff from the comments, by the way, though that would spoil the feels aspect a bit if actually included.)

Actually it wouldn't
Think of it. The poor drone feels it's accomplished a noble purpose, while all it did is deliver a piece of shit intergalactic reblog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted this based on my enjoyment of it, and its high quality. Plus the feels.

But I feel it didn't pay off enough. I reached the end and thought "that's it?"

The message is really the problem here. It's superbly written, but feels… derivative. Unfortunately I can't be specific as to what I feel it's derivative of - so perhaps I rephrase that and say that I found it predictable. Specifically, I was able to predict basically everything about it, content-wise. I didn't hate it because it was well-written but that doesn't change my opinion that it just wasn't particularly interesting.

And regarding similar articles… well, take SCP-1342. The actual author of that article feels this is the superior take on the 'alien message' concept, but I disagree; 1342 brings in a rather unexpected twist. It's not the biggest shocker in the world, but it's still interesting and quite compelling. And Astronomical… Well, the first two-thirds are slow and kind of "eh", but holy shit that ending. It's written to give you goosebumps, and it does.

Whereas here you've got some pretty good feels going on, and impeccable writing, but… The rest is "eh". And since the message is almost as central to the article as the feels, that's a problem.

I'm still upvoting because I enjoyed it and to me the +1 or -1 is given depending on whether or not I enjoyed it. but I hope you consider taking another spin on the message section at least.

(I absolutely love the spam/reblog stuff from the comments, by the way, though that would spoil the feels aspect a bit if actually included.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't that one deleted, or have you guys been hiding something from me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just barely like this enough to upvote, and a lot of that came from the implication (from the discussion, but that's good enough) that this could be interplanetary spam instead of a dying race's final wishes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit, you hit me right in my crying-for-dead-Mars-Rovers feels.

item.title: Re: Inaccuracy
item.content:encoded: It's still in space during the interview.

item.title: Inaccuracy
item.content:encoded: I liked this article. But one quick criticism: 70k is -203 degrees celcius… I'm not sure if this was intentional, or if it is still is in space during the interview…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last interview sold me. It is a nice story. +1

item.title: Re: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: I didn't upvote until I read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And a system that allows the teleportation of manned spacecraft to Pluto in a matter of hours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is everything I love bundled into one SCP.

Yes, yes please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My feels. Ok, I won't tell nothing besides that. +1

E: I tried to use a dalek voice for Harbinger…

I shouldn't have done that… I'd upvote again If I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

Ladies and gentlemen, proof that the nice ones can still work.

Well done, thou good and faithful servant… ;.;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was all set not to be impressed by this.

Then I got to the last log.

I…

Yes, Harbinger. You're a good boy. ;_; You go rest now, your mission is over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, keep in mind that these are not all written in the 'present'.

Also, sunday drive to the moon? Bah, we have a teleport kiddie pool! =)

item.title: 99% of Sentient Races will not do this
item.content:encoded: If you read this message PLEASE SEND IT BACK TO SPACE. 5 other sentient races must listen to this message, or else your galaxy will spin towards death! If you send it to 5 other sentient races, you will meet the love of your life. IT REALLY WORKS! Our planet has collapsed towards death because we didn't propogate it in time. DO NOT MAKE OUR MISTAKES. Please forward this message to every sentient race you know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is some seriously advanced space technology the Foundation has. Like 'let's take a Sunday drive to the Moon' advanced, especially if this is 'standard' for your unnamed SCP. I'm having trouble fitting it into my head-cannon, considering there are several SCP objects which are irretrievable due to being on Mars or the Moon.

Instead of continuing to nay-say on this point, I will take it as a sign that we need more objects from space.

Circumference, or the outer perimeter of a circular/elliptical object, is probably not the word you're looking for. That would make this thing three times longer than it is wide, and that's not bringing up a 'teardrop' shape in my head. More of a cigar.

There are a couple instances where word-choice seems to break up the flow of the document, such as

And in the end, with the… I guess you'd call them interview logs? I'm having trouble understanding why this far-flung object needs containment, especially now that it's inactive, broken, and dead.

While I like that we're doing more space stuff, this object doesn't grab me. No vote right now. Maybe in a few days I'll stumble on it again and change my mind.

e: My criticism above is less valid with my better understanding of site culture and how SCPs are supposed to be written/read. I'm going to change by neutrality to a +1.
